title: Microsoft IIS Client Certificate Authentication Failures
dataSources: `index=web` `sourcetype=ms:iis:auto` with SSL client cert auth (403.7 patterns) and optional `iis:httperr`
app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (`Splunk_TA_windows`); Splunk Add-on for Microsoft IIS (Splunkbase 3185)
spl:
index=web sourcetype="ms:iis:auto"
| where sc_status=403 AND match(cs_uri_stem, "(?i)\.asmx|api")
| regex _raw="(?i)(403\.7|certificate|SSL client)"
| stats count by c_ip, cs_uri_stem, sc_status
| where count > 10
description: Mutual TLS and client certificate mapping failures often surface as HTTP 403.7-class patterns. Volume spikes frequently track partner certificate expiries or cipher suite mismatches after hardening.
value: Protects B2B APIs and federated integrations that depend on certificate authentication continuity.
implementation: Enable detailed errors in lower env only; prod relies on W3C fields. Join `iis:httperr` for `BadRequest` reasons.
visualization: Stacked bars for status/substatus, Perfmon timecharts, top client tables.
grandmaExplanation: We catch client certificate auth breaks that block partners and APIs.
